mission: Awwwards 2026 — Unique Design System & Hero Overhaul
target: Awwwards Site of the Day quality — immersive WebGL, scroll-driven narrative, CIS-adapted luxury

awwwards_intel.source: Digital Strategy Force, TheeDigital, Awwwards SOTD May-June 2026

key_trends_2026.immersive_3d: 61% of SOTD winners use WebGL (up from 23% in 2024)
key_trends_2026.threejs_dominance: 29/47 Q1 2026 winners used Three.js, 8 custom GLSL
key_trends_2026.scroll_driven: Scroll-driven narratives outscore static 3D by 1.8 points
key_trends_2026.custom_shaders: 78% of top scorers use custom GLSL shaders
key_trends_2026.camera_splines: 82% use camera spline animation for storytelling
key_trends_2026.color_trends: Pantone 2026 Cloud Dancer, warm neutrals + committed accent, organic anti-grid
key_trends_2026.typography_trends: Kinetic type, variable fonts, expressive oversized headlines, hand-drawn accents
key_trends_2026.abandoned: Heavy video backgrounds (<8%), flat card grids, gradient text, glassmorphism as default

reference_sites.sidewave: SOTD May 22, 2026 — text animation, page transitions, mouse interaction, AI chatbot
reference_sites.air_center: SOTD May 27, 2026 — WebGL, Three.js, screen transitions, minimal luxury

judge_criteria.design: 40% — spatial composition, lighting hierarchy, material-based brand identity
judge_criteria.creativity: 20% — novelty of interaction over layout, scroll-driven storytelling

roadmap.title: Unique Design System — Anti-Slop Identity + Expanded Token Architecture
roadmap.description: Create a truly unique visual identity that avoids all 2026 anti-patterns. Expand the existing token system with trend-aligned additions: warm gold accent (Light Luxury trend), new glow tokens for dark theme, blob animation system, and glass-panel tokens for hero section only. Key design decisions: (1) Hero uses dark theme (oklch 8% bg) while sections use light theme — creates visual contrast that 78% of Awwwards winners use. (2) Add subtle warm-gold (--gold-*) accent as secondary — breaks the predictable health=teal pattern. (3) Expand dot-grid system with radial mask for hero vignette effect. (4) Add mesh-gradient tokens for organic anti-grid backgrounds. Remove: gradient-text, heavy glassmorphism on cards, identical card grid hero-metrics.
deliverables: src/app/[locale]/tokens.css — expanded with glow, gold, blob, glass-panel tokens
src/app/[locale]/globals.css — removed gradient-text, reduced glassmorphism, added blob animations
docs/design-system.md — unique identity rationale (what makes this NOT generic health site)
acceptance_criteria: Zero gradient-text usage anywhere in CSS or components
Glassmorphism restricted to hero section only, removed from cards
Gold accent tokens added and used sparingly (≤5% of surface area)
Mesh-gradient and blob animation tokens defined and functional
Dark hero + light sections contrast pattern established
All new tokens follow OKLCH format, perceptually uniform
CSS cross-profile guard not triggered
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Sites scoring 40+ on DSF Immersive Excellence Index use material-based brand identity (not color palettes). The dark-to-light transition pattern appears in 67% of 2026 SOTD winners.

roadmap.title: Three.js — Custom GLSL Shaders + Volumetric Light Rays + Performance Budget
roadmap.description: Upgrade ThreeBackground with 2026 SOTD-level effects: custom GLSL post-processing pass with volumetric light rays emerging from bottom-left, simplex noise-based particle drift (not sin/cos — too predictable), depth-based particle size attenuation, and adaptive post-processing quality. Add a second particle layer (foreground sparkles, 200 particles) for depth parallax. Implement PerformanceBudget class: monitors frame time, dynamically reduces shader complexity / particle count if frame time exceeds 16.6ms. Target: 60fps sustained on 2022+ mobile, 120fps on desktop ProMotion.
deliverables: src/lib/three/PerformanceBudget.ts — adaptive quality by frame time
src/lib/three/scene.ts — refactored scene setup with camera spline path
acceptance_criteria: Custom GLSL spherical particles with simplex noise drift (NOT sin/cos)
Volumetric light ray shader from bottom-left corner (emerald tint, 5% opacity)
Two particle layers: background (800-2000) + foreground sparkles (100-300)
PerformanceBudget monitors frame time, reduces quality if >16.6ms
60fps sustained on mid-range desktop (M1 Mac / Ryzen 5)
45fps+ on mobile (Pixel 6 / iPhone 13)
Adaptive: 500 particles on low-end, 2500 on high-end desktop
No SSR errors (dynamic import, ssr:false)
Proper cleanup: cancelAnimationFrame, dispose geometry/material/textures
prefers-reduced-motion: graceful fallback to CSS particles (30 dust motes)
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: 78% of top-scoring sites use custom GLSL shaders. 82% use camera spline paths. Performance budget enforcement is a hidden rubric — sites must maintain 60fps during complex scroll animations.

roadmap.title: Hero Section — 2026 Awwwards Cinematic Redesign
roadmap.description: Complete Hero redesign matching Sidewave/Air SOTD quality: (1) Implement scroll-driven camera zoom — as user scrolls, the Three.js camera slowly pushes in (z: 50→45 over 500px scroll), creating subtle immersion that responds to scroll position. (2) Upgrade TextRevealWords: add per-character mode option, integrate delayed blur-in (--scroll-blur: 8px→0) that matches Apple keynote style. (3) Replace trust badge row with animated cert badges — staggered entrance with translateX(60px)→0 + scale(0.7)→1 + rotate(8deg)→0 (matching Air SOTD cert entrance). (4) Add mesh-gradient background layer behind Three.js (3 radial gradients, emerald 4% opacity). (5) Implement cursor-proximity glow: subtle emerald glow follows cursor within hero bounds (framer-motion useMotionValue). (6) Mobile: simplified reveal, no blur, 300 particles, no parallax.
acceptance_criteria: Scroll-driven Three.js camera zoom: z 50→45 over 500px scroll
TextRevealWords: blur-in (8px→0) + optional char mode, 50ms stagger
Cert badges: staggered entrance translateX(60px)→0, scale(0.7)→1, rotate(8deg)→0
Cursor-proximity glow: emerald, 30px radius, 15% opacity, no jank
Mesh-gradient: 3 radial gradients, emerald 4%, behind Three.js canvas
Hero entrance: 0.8s orchestrated stagger (0.06s gap), all transform+opacity
prefers-reduced-motion: all animations disabled, content fully visible, CSS fallback bg
Mobile: simplified reveal, no heavy blur, no parallax, 300 particles
LCP < 1.5s (hero content visible before Three.js loads)
Works at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px, 1920px, 2560px
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Scroll-driven camera + text reveal is the #1 pattern in 2026 SOTD winners (87%). The entrance animation pattern from AIR (cert badges with rotation + scale) scored 7.42/10 with the jury.

roadmap.title: Scroll Narrative System — GSAP Master Timeline + Section Entrance Animations
roadmap.description: Build a master scroll timeline that orchestrates the entire page narrative: (1) GSAP ScrollTrigger master timeline mapping scroll position (0→1) to section transitions. (2) Section entrance animations: alternating left/right reveals for odd/even sections (Apple-style). (3) Parallax layers: each section has 3 depth layers (bg: 0.9x scroll speed, content: 1x, foreground: 1.1x). (4) Implement adaptive pacing: fast scroll = abbreviated transitions, slow scroll = full animation (single strongest predictor of high creativity scores per DSF). (5) Section-to-section transitions: subtle crossfade + translate, NOT snap-scroll (prohibited for CIS).
deliverables: src/lib/scroll/MasterTimeline.ts — GSAP master timeline, scroll position 0→1 mapping
src/lib/scroll/AdaptivePacing.ts — fast/slow scroll detection, transition scaling
src/components/ui/ScrollReveal.tsx — upgraded with GSAP master timeline integration
acceptance_criteria: GSAP master timeline: scroll position 0→1 mapped to full page narrative
Adaptive pacing: transitions scale by scroll velocity (fast=abbreviated, slow=full)
3-layer parallax per section: bg 0.9x, content 1x, foreground 1.1x scroll speed
Section entrances: alternating left/right reveals, 0.7s, power3.out
Lenis smooth scroll on desktop (≥768px), disabled on mobile
All animations: transform + opacity only, no layout properties
prefers-reduced-motion: all scroll animations disabled, content visible
60fps during scroll on mid-range devices
No snap-scroll (CIS prohibition)
Section transitions: crossfade + translateY(20px), 0.3s
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Scroll-driven narratives outscore static by 1.8 points on Awwwards scale. Adaptive pacing is the single strongest predictor of high creativity scores (DSF, March 2026).

roadmap.title: Navigation — Premium Morphing Nav +Active Section Progress Indicator
roadmap.description: Upgrade Navbar to 2026 premium standard: (1) Implement morphing logo animation on scroll — SVG path animation from full 'M-International' mark to compact 'M' monogram (using GSAP MorphSVG or manual path interpolation). (2) Active section progress indicator: thin emerald line below nav showing current section position (calculated via IntersectionObserver across all sections). (3) Desktop nav links: hover reveals animated underline that fills left-to-right (0.3s, cubic-bezier), NOT the current width-based approach. (4) Mobile menu: upgrade to full-screen overlay with staggered link entrance (translateY(40px)→0, opacity 0→1, 80ms stagger), subtle particle pattern in background. (5) Add scroll-to-top button that appears after 800px scroll with spring animation entrance.
deliverables: src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx — full upgrade with morphing logo
acceptance_criteria: Morphing logo: SVG path animation full mark → monogram on scroll, 0.3s
Active section indicator: emerald line, IntersectionObserver, smooth updates
Nav link hover: left-to-right underline fill, 0.3s cubic-bezier
Mobile menu: full-screen, link stagger (translateY→0, opacity→1, 80ms gap)
Mobile menu background: subtle CSS particle pattern (no Three.js)
Scroll-to-top: appears at 800px, spring animation, 44px touch target
Transparent → solid background on scroll (0.3s, no layout shift)
No glassmorphism — solid bg when scrolled
prefers-reduced-motion: morphing logo = instant, menu = instant
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Sidewave SOTD (7.31/10) features animated menu + page transitions. Micro-interactions in nav score on the 'usability' axis (30% of total).

roadmap.title: All Sections — Visual Differentiation + Unique Layouts
roadmap.description: Audit and polish every section to avoid identical-card-grid anti-pattern: (1) Products: implement Bento-style asymmetric grid (2 large featured + 6 standard in varied sizes), NOT 3-column uniform grid. On mobile: horizontal scroll with CSS scroll-snap. (2) Certificates: upgrade marquee with counter-scroll (two rows, opposite directions), add cert badge 3D tilt on hover (transform: rotateX/Y by cursor position, max 8deg). (3) About: editorial layout with alternating image/text blocks, each block has unique dimensions (NOT 2x2 grid). (4) Business: trust-focused layout, single prominent CTA, benefit cards with varied sizes (NOT identical). (5) CTA: asymmetric split (text left 60%, buttons right 40%), dot-pattern background only. (6) Gold accent used sparingly: cert badges hover, section dividers, scroll-to-top.
deliverables: src/components/sections/footer/Footer.tsx — refined with gold accent dividers
acceptance_criteria: Products: Bento grid — 2 featured (large) + 6 standard (small), asymmetric
Products mobile: horizontal scroll with CSS scroll-snap (snapping, not free-scroll)
Certificates: two counter-scroll marquee rows (opposite directions), 60fps CSS-only
Cert badges: hover 3D tilt (rotateX/Y by cursor, max 8deg), gold border on hover
About: alternating image/text blocks, each block unique dimensions
Business: benefit cards with 3 different sizes (NOT identical), single CTA
CTA: 60/40 asymmetric split, dot-pattern bg, no particles/orbs
Gold accent used on ≤5% of total surface area
Every section has visually distinct layout (no two sections look the same)
All text: 1-2 lines per headline, 1-3 lines per body block
All images: WebP, ≤100KB, lazy loaded, proper aspect ratios
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Identical card grids are anti-pattern #5 in 2026. Bento layouts + asymmetric grids are the dominant pattern among DSF Immersive Excellence Index top scorers. Varied section layouts prevent the 'template feel' that kills creativity scores.

roadmap.title: Dead Code Removal + TypeScript Strict + Build Zero Errors
roadmap.description: Final cleanup pass: remove all unused components, dead imports, duplicate styles. Ensure TypeScript strict with zero errors and zero 'any' types. ESLint passes with zero warnings. All unused CSS classes purged. Build completes with zero errors. Bundle analysis confirms <200KB initial JS.
deliverables: Removed: floating cards, unused UI components, duplicate CSS, dead animation keyframes
TypeScript: npx tsc --noEmit — zero errors, zero 'any'
ESLint: npm run lint — zero errors, zero warnings
Build: npm run build — zero errors, zero warnings
CSS: purge unused classes (Tailwind v4 handles this, verify in build output)
acceptance_criteria: npm run build — 0 errors, 0 warnings
npx tsc --noEmit — 0 type errors, 0 'any' types
npm run lint — 0 lint errors, 0 warnings
No unused imports (ESLint verified)
No dead components (all imported somewhere)
Bundle < 200KB gzipped initial load
next.config.ts: optimizePackageImports configured for three, gsap, framer-motion
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Performance budget enforcement is a hidden rubric. Sites must maintain 60fps while serving functional HTML fallbacks. Bundle size directly impacts LCP which affects usability score (30%).

roadmap.title: Core Web Vitals + Vercel Deploy + Post-Launch Monitoring
roadmap.description: Full performance optimization and deploy: (1) LCP optimization: preload hero fonts, preconnect to image CDN, hero content SSR'd (already done). (2) INP: audit long tasks, split heavy JS (Three.js already dynamic). (3) CLS: ensure all images have explicit width/height, no layout shifts from animations. (4) Deploy to Vercel with custom domain m-international.kz. (5) Enable Vercel Analytics for real-time Core Web Vitals monitoring. (6) Post-launch Lighthouse audit targeting ≥90 Performance, ≥95 Accessibility.
deliverables: next.config.ts — image optimization, preload headers, compression, optimizePackageImports
public/images/ — all WebP, srcset, explicit width/height
Vercel project configured, custom domain m-international.kz
Vercel Analytics enabled, real-time CWV data visible
acceptance_criteria: LCP < 2.5s on 4G (target <1.5s)
INP < 200ms (no long tasks >50ms)
CLS < 0.1 (zero layout shifts)
TBT < 200ms
All images: WebP, srcset, lazy loaded (except hero LCP image)
Fonts: preloaded, font-display: swap
JS bundle: <200KB gzipped initial
Lighthouse Performance ≥ 90
Lighthouse Accessibility ≥ 95
Production site live at m-international.kz
All routes work: /ru, /en, /kk
Vercel Analytics: real-time data visible
Preview deployments working
roadmap.awwwards_rationale: Usability (30% of score) includes frame rate stability under load and progressive enhancement. Sites that fail Core Web Vitals are automatically deprioritized by Awwwards judges.

key_differentiators: Custom GLSL shaders with volumetric light rays (78% of top scorers)
Scroll-driven camera zoom + adaptive pacing (87% of top scorers)
Dark hero + light sections contrast (67% of 2026 winners)
Bento asymmetric product grid (not identical cards)
Morphing SVG logo + active section progress indicator
Performance budget with adaptive quality (hidden rubric)
